app: HTTP Event Collector from regulatory intelligence vendor or manual curator tool
spl:
index=grc sourcetype="reg:change_item" earliest=-730d
| eval eff=strptime(effective_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where eff>now() OR eff>relative_time(now(),"-90d@d")
| timechart span=30d sum(impact_score) as rolling_impact by jurisdiction
| trendline sma3(rolling_impact) as impact_trend
description: Laws and supervisory guidance change continuously. Scoring incoming regulatory change items by how many in-scope controls they touch—and trending that score—helps compliance prioritize policy updates, training, and control amendments before effective dates.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001, SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes reg:change_item. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for Compliance Trending — Regulatory Change Feed Impact Score on In-Scope Controls. We watch trends so leaders see improvement, not a one-time snapshot.
implementation: (1) Maintain mapping table from `impacted_control_ids` to owners; (2) human validates `impact_score`—do not fully automate legal severity; (3) integrate effective-date countdown alerts; (4) deduplicate vendor spam with `item_id`; (5) cross-link to policy management tickets. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=reg:change_item`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: HTTP Event Collector from regulatory intelligence vendor or manual curator tool.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype="reg:change_item"` (item_id, jurisdiction, effective_date, summary_tokens, impacted_control_ids, impact_score, _time).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain mapping table from `impacted_control_ids` to owners; (2) human validates `impact_score`—do not fully automate legal severity; (3) integrate effective-date countdown alerts; (4) deduplicate vendor spam with `item_id`; (5) cross-link to policy management tickets.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="reg:change_item" earliest=-730d
| eval eff=strptime(effective_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where eff>now() OR eff>relative_time(now(),"-90d@d")
| timechart span=30d sum(impact_score) as rolling_impact by jurisdiction
| trendline sma3(rolling_impact) as impact_trend
```

Understanding this SPL

**Compliance Trending — Regulatory Change Feed Impact Score on In-Scope Controls** — Laws and supervisory guidance change continuously. Scoring incoming regulatory change items by how many in-scope controls they touch—and trending that score—helps compliance prioritize policy updates, training, and control amendments before effective dates.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype="reg:change_item"` (item_id, jurisdiction, effective_date, summary_tokens, impacted_control_ids, impact_score, _time). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HTTP Event Collector from regulatory intelligence vendor or manual curator tool. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: reg:change_item. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype="reg:change_item", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **eff** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where eff>now() OR eff>relative_time(now(),"-90d@d")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=30d** buckets with a separate series **by jurisdiction** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Output fields: eff.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (rolling_impact + impact_trend), Table (upcoming items), Single value (next-90d sum).
visualization: Line chart (rolling_impact + impact_trend), Table (upcoming items), Single value (next-90d sum). Key fields: `eff`.
knownFalsePositives: Trends shift during new framework adoption, scope changes, or after major audit findings remediation.
